channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Norway and Arunachal Pradesh Collaborate on River Kinetic Energy Project
item.description: The Arunachal Pradesh government has signed a memorandum of understanding with Norway’s Tidal Sails AS to establish India’s first kinetic energy demonstration plant. This 500 kW project will generate ...

item.title: Steve Mkasi Clarifies Viral Video Misinterpretation After Roadside Confrontation
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal Athletics President Steve Mkasi has addressed claims that a viral video showing him grappling with another man on a roadway was misinterpreted. The footage, which circulated widely on so...

item.title: APC seeks Senator Hanga's support after claims of mistreatment by Kwankwaso
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) in Kano State has intensified efforts to persuade Senator Rufai Sani Hanga, who represents Kano Central, to defect to the ruling party. The APC delegation, led by S...

item.title: Anne Hathaway confirms pregnancy and shares newborn's name
item.description: Anne Hathaway, the acclaimed actress known for her roles in films such as 'The Dark Knight' and 'Les Misérables', has announced her pregnancy with her second child. The news was shared through her soc...

item.title: Teen Killer Still Enjoys Violence, Legal Battle Over Infectious Disease Lawsuit
item.description: The article highlights three key legal cases. First, a teenager who killed his grandmother in a random attack is facing sentencing, with both the Crown and his defense lawyer requesting the maximum pe...

item.title: BIR Box and Tax Secrecy in Sara Duterte's Impeachment Case
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, focusing on the sealed BIR box containing her tax records. The box, which has moved between the House, Senate, and B...

item.title: Boomanulla Oval Steps Closer to Aboriginal Community Control
item.description: Boomanulla Oval, a culturally significant site in Canberra's inner south, is progressing toward returning to Aboriginal community control. An interim management committee, comprising community and gov...

item.title: Eskom Addresses Power Outage at Somerset Mall Due to Infrastructure Theft
item.description: The Somerset Mall in Cape Town, along with surrounding industrial areas, has experienced a prolonged electricity outage lasting three consecutive days following the theft of critical high-voltage elec...

item.title: Trump's Influence Over Lindsey Graham: Insights from His Biographer
item.description: The article explores claims by Donald Trump's biographer, Michael Wolff, that the former president may have leveraged his knowledge of U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham's sexuality to exert influence over h...

item.title: Spain Defeats France 2-0 to Advance to 2026 World Cup Final
item.description: Spain secured a 2-0 victory over France in a thrilling World Cup match at Dallas stadium, advancing to the final. Goals from Mikel Oryazabal and Pedro Porro sealed the win, with Spain dominating the g...

item.title: Kent Carpenter: Marine Biologist's Legacy in Philippine Marine Research and South China Sea Case
item.description: Dr. Kent Carpenter, an internationally renowned American marine biologist, dedicated his career to studying the Philippines' rich marine biodiversity. His pioneering research on coral reefs and marine...

item.title: Melania Trump's Aide Faces Mockery for Controversial Claim on NewsMax
item.description: Senior aide to First Lady Melania Trump, Marc Beckman, faced widespread ridicule after making a controversial statement during an interview on NewsMax. Beckman claimed Melania Trump is 'the most conse...

item.title: Campfire Bans Expand Across Vancouver Island Amid Rising Wildfire Threats
item.description: A new campfire ban has been implemented across most of Vancouver Island due to escalating wildfire risks. The decision, announced by local authorities, follows a surge in extreme weather conditions an...

item.title: Haryana farmers and workers protest against government's power distribution reforms
item.description: Farmers, electricity employees, workers, and consumer organisations in Haryana have launched a statewide agitation against the government's proposal to grant a parallel electricity distribution licenc...

item.title: Tanzania's Tourism and Transport Sectors Drive Economic Growth
item.description: Tanzania's tourism sector has become a key driver of economic growth, with international tourist arrivals rising by 5.9% to 2.3 million in the year ending June 2026. This increase generated higher spe...

item.title: California Lawmakers Urge Quebec to Lift U.S. Alcohol Restrictions Amid Trade Tensions
item.description: California legislators from both political parties have signed a letter urging Quebec Premier Christine Frechette to resume U.S. wine and spirits sales despite Canada's trade war with the U.S. Rep. Mi...

item.title: Impeachment trial highlights VP Sara Duterte's potential role in alleged threats against Marcos family
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte has sparked discussions about her possible involvement in an alleged assassination threat against President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. and his...

item.title: ICPC Chairman Urges Combating Corruption and Educating Youth for Nigeria's Future
item.description: The Chairman of Nigeria's Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC), Dr Musa Adamu Aliyu, emphasized that lasting peace in Nigeria is impossible without addressing cor...

item.title: Bank of Ghana Revokes Zeepay's License for Regulatory Non-Compliance
item.description: The Bank of Ghana (BoG) has revoked the Dedicated Electronic Money Issuer (DEMI) license of Zeepay Ghana Limited, citing multiple regulatory breaches and the company’s failure to comply with central b...

item.title: Economic Policies and Political Statements
